latestCycle.goal: improve the late Andes mature-window precipitation split by blending local terrain-normal flow with a short broad-terrain sample
latestCycle.verifiedImprovement: default terrainDirectionalBlend is now 0.05, improving Andes precipRatio from 1.0099 to 1.0394 at 75600 and from 0.9226 to 0.9271 at 105480 while Rockies and Himalaya-Tibet stayed windward-favored and the validation suite still passed

blockingGaps: broader Earth-like realism still needs fresh mature-window re-audits in the live app with the new broad-terrain sampling blend active across circulation, clouds, storms, and precipitation placement

orographic realism improved but is not solved: the Andes mature-window precipRatio now clears 1.0 at 75600 but is still only 0.9271 at 105480, and the terrain/flow interaction still needs more selective refinement
runtime smoothness is still not signed off; the latest live run now has healthy telemetry and still reports earth_update_p95_high, earth_update_max_high, and wind_targets_failing
world-class status still requires realism and smoothness to pass in the same fresh live run
cycleInputs: latest runtime summary from npm run agent:summarize-runtime-log

browserObservation.note: reused the canonical localhost solo-mode tab, kept the single-page-tab path clean, and the run still emitted non-empty runtime telemetry

defaultNextPriority: stay on orographic realism and re-audit the Andes with the new broad-terrain sampling blend active, prioritizing the remaining later-window shortfall at 105480
target the next physics cycle at one concrete src-side fix area before taking any new diagnostics-only detour
use browser verification only after an offline candidate looks promising, and keep it to one canonical localhost run
when smoothness becomes the blocker again, use the fresh non-empty runtime telemetry before changing renderer or worker performance behavior
keep validation on the clean world-class checkout only
